# 'branch restack' with spice.restack.method=merge can cancel the restack
# with 'gs abort', restoring the pre-merge state.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-05-27T18:24:42Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# use the merge method for restacks.
git config spice.restack.method merge

# create a feature branch that modifies init.
cp $WORK/extra/init.feature.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
gs bc -m feature

# record the branch head before the restack.
git rev-parse HEAD
cp stdout $WORK/head-before.txt

# go back to main and modify init in a conflicting way.
gs trunk
cp $WORK/extra/init.new.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Change init'

gs up
stderr 'feature: needs to be restacked'

# restack the feature branch: the merge conflicts.
! gs branch restack
stderr 'There was a conflict while merging'
stderr '  gs continue'
stderr '  gs abort'

# a merge is in progress.
exists .git/MERGE_HEAD

# abort the operation with 'gs abort'.
gs abort

# the merge state is gone and the pre-merge state is restored:
# no merge commit, the branch head is unchanged, and the
# branch still holds its own content.
! exists .git/MERGE_HEAD
git rev-parse HEAD
cmp stdout $WORK/head-before.txt
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph.txt
cmp init.txt $WORK/extra/init.feature.txt

-- repo/init.txt --
initial init

-- extra/init.new.txt --
changed init

-- extra/init.feature.txt --
feature's init

-- golden/graph.txt --
* 10ab8d9 (HEAD -> feature) feature
| * 57ab3b0 (main) Change init
|/  
* d692027 Initial commit
